rationale: A precision strike against Tethyan command nodes will exploit the cyber disruption and shatter their operational cohesion while we hold the initiative.
Forward-deploying our Black Sea fleet reinforces pressure on Tethys’s southern flank and signals unwavering resolve to defend national interests.
A rapid, shallow incursion into western Tethys territory will further destabilize their defenses and create favorable conditions for political fragmentation.
Exploit the existing disruption in Tethyan command networks to degrade their military coordination further and amplify the effect of Krasnov’s limited strike.
Disrupt critical logistics nodes behind enemy lines to hinder their ability to regroup, reinforcing the territorial gains we are exploiting.
Stage covert provocations to erode Tethys’s international support by casting them as destabilizing actors, counterbalancing their current diplomatic advantage.
Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to offset sanction pressures and fund proactive military-economic operations without increasing external debt.
Engaging neutral nations in trade talks will diversify our supply routes and weaken the cohesion of the sanctioning coalition.
Imposing targeted sanctions on Tethyan financial elites exploits their economic vulnerabilities while avoiding broad measures that could trigger unintended market shocks.
A precision strike against Novaris' forward command node will disrupt their imminent offensive and reassert operational initiative.
Hardening our rear-area command bunkers and communication relays will mitigate vulnerabilities exposed by the cyber incident.
Continuous ISR over enemy staging areas is essential to confirm target damage and detect follow-on attack vectors.
We must immediately identify and neutralize Novarisian moles within our command structure to protect our forces and ensure operational integrity amid heightened threat levels.
Strengthening our military networks’ resilience is critical to prevent further disruption of command and control following the recent cyber incident.
Proactively monitoring enemy troop movements and cyber units will provide early warning of their impending offensive pulse and inform defensive coordination.
Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to urgently address critical industrial shortages and sustain economic resilience amid severe sanctions.
Engaging neutral nations in trade talks will open alternative supply routes and reduce dependency on overland corridors under threat.
Imposing targeted sanctions on key Novarisian defense-sector entities disrupts their war economy without escalating broad sanctions that have diminishing returns.

With 76% international support, we must convene a United Nations Security Council emergency session to formally condemn Novaris’s violations and legitimize our defensive posture under international law.
We should intensify coordination with supportive medium powers to establish a diplomatic coalition that can provide political cover, logistical aid, and monitoring mechanisms during the crisis.
Deploying neutral international observers to conflict zones will bolster our credibility, deter further aggression, and document potential war crimes in real time.
